name: Making The World A Better Place, One Step At A Time
description: This is a discussion group oriented towards those who have felt an itch, a pull, maybe even a twinge of guilt, towards getting more involved in the work of making the world a better place and would like to figure out their next steps with other like-minded folks.

name: Design Of Tools For Thought
description:
How do different tools for thought affect our thinking and can we design new ones? Let's learn!

Rolling application

name: Family History Research: An Introduction
description:
An introductory class on the fundamentals of doing genealogical research to learn about the lives of your ancestors and those who came before as a way of finding identity and belonging.

Rolling application

name: Culture As Data
description: Algorithms read our culture before we do. What you see on Netflix, Spotify, and Audible is chosen by systems built to model taste and predict your next click, and the books, music, and art we love are shoveled into training data for massive AI models. This course shows you how that works by teaching you to do it: you'll turn a body of work you care about into data and analyze thousands of items at once. You'll see what that reveals, and what is lost when the human reader is replaced by a machine. Through a guided project, you'll investigate a question of your own and build it into a finished piece of research.

name: The Dawn Of Everything: A Guided Reading
description: Join us to learn why "this is just how things are" is the biggest lie ever told about human history.
when:

name: Short Stories: The Mundane And The Magic
description:
In this class, we will read stories that transform the mundane into something magical—whether through meaning shining a light on the everyday, or the quotidian becoming an entirely strange thing. We will then take what we learn to develop our own story.

Application deadline: Jun 24

name: Essay Club!
description: This class is an essay club, specifically an essay-reading club. Think of it as a normal reading club... but with essays. This is for you if you want a little more lively discussion in your life. I'm teaching this class because I want that, and I want to widen my perspectives on life!

A class to demystify the law and study how it can be used for revolutionary change.

name: San Diego Microseason Corps
description:
This is an invitation to help create the calendar of microseasons that will thoughtfully mark the passage of time in San Diego. The corps will take short, detail-oriented walks in various San Diego neighborhoods with the intent to learn about our region's biocultural character and its role in our communities through field journaling. You will be a citizen scientist contributing to a grassroots database that has the potential to inform environmental insights, tradition, and ways of being for our city.

Rolling application
when:
Saturdays 10:30-11:30am,
Jul 11-Aug 1
4 meetings

Following this, San Diego Microseason Corps will meet once a month indefinitely.

name: Meditation And Embodiment (Apply by July 11)
description:
Each session, we’ll try one mindbody practice together, then discuss it or other stuff we’ve been practicing recently.

Rolling application

name: Public Power 101
description:
Public Power 101 is a popular education style, four-week class that explores how San Diegans can fight the rising cost of living and build a not-for-profit publicly owned alternative to SDGE through community organizing and public education.

Application deadline: Jul 21

name: Understanding AI: From Philosophical Underpinnings To Practical Tips
description:
We will look at AI, especially LLMs, from a variety of perspectives -- including, but not limited to philosophical, historical, cognitive, and technical -- to deeply understand it and gain practical knowledge of how to use it skillfully and wisely.

Application deadline: Jul 27

name: Let’s Talk Crypto
description:
What are Bitcoin and Ethereum, really? Does crypto enable cool stuff or just shitcoins and scams?

Application deadline: Aug 6

name: Harm Reduction Is Survival
description:
This class is a radical & practical introduction to harm reduction as a life-saving response to the drug war, criminalization, and abandonment of people who use drugs. Students will learn how different drugs affect the body, how to respond to overdoses, and how harm reduction principles challenge abstinence-only, carceral, and moralized approaches to drug use.
